segment-text: Bill and Ted Face the Music
We hear the VOICE of a very, very OLD MAN: VERY, VERY OLD MAN (V.O.)
DOCUMENTARY FOOTAGE: CLOSE UP: VIDEO FOOTAGE OF BILL AND TED, 17 YEARS OLD, from the beginning of "Excellent Adventure."
I'm Bill S Preston Esquire!
And I am Ted "Theodore" Logan. together we are..
Wyld Stallyns!!

CUT TO: BILL AND TED AT 19 - doing their guitar solo at the end of "Bogus Journey" - the super-fast, virtuosic riffing.. SHOT: PEOPLE FROM ALL OVER THE WORLD, watching. CUT TO: NOT LONG AFTER THAT (circa 1992): Bill and Ted, excited, wide-eyed - the picture of youthful optimism. Being interviewed by KURT LODER on MTV.
In other music news.. Claiming they have been told by "a civilization seven hundred years in the future" that their music will "do nothing less than save the world.."
Now on MTV we see a PHOTO OF BILL AND TED, age 20.
Wyld Stallyns has just put down a hundred thousand dollars to reserve the famous "Fabulous Forum" for a date twenty years from now..
CUT TO: DISTANT, HAND-HELD VIDEO FOOTAGE of the guys handing a ceremonial 4 foot check to the "FABULOUS FORUM" BOOKING MANAGER. The footage is shaky and distant.
See you all here in twenty years - to celebrate two decades of triumphance!
How's it goin', future? See you soon!
20 year old Bill and Ted turn to each other, do a little air guitar. FREEZE on that image - and HOLD, with ominous music. THE BAND VAN HALEN
The only problem was - and it turns out to be a pretty big problem..
They never came up with a song.
The song, let's say. Not to mention the record. Or any record, really.
THE BAND IRON MAIDEN
They got record deals - we know that cuz they paid us a ton to just basically be session guys.
But no matter what we played, it was never good enough.
Yeah - cuz like, they kept saying to us:
"This has to save the world."
QUICK SHOTS:
"No, Jeff - this has to save the world."
And I remember saying: "Guys, it's a song." The Beatles didn't save the world. Mozart didn't save the world.
"Save the world, save the world.."

Clapton rolls his eyes and makes the "jerk off" motion. FOOTAGE FROM "BOGUS JOURNEY:" Bill and Ted, on stage, with their wives to their side and their babies on their backs-BILL (to crowd; re his son) Say hello to Little Ted!
(same) And Little Bill!
18 years old now. "WILL LOGAN AND THEO PRESTON."
Okay, like, our dads - they were told - in high school - that their music was gonna save the world.
I mean that's a lot of pressure. Like, if somebody came to us, and told us that our music was gonna save the world? WILL Well that would be pretty okay.
Yeah, I'd be good with that.
CUT TO: THEIR WIVES, now. ELIZABETH LOGAN." Title: "JOANNA PRESTON AND
Look, the point is, they took it seriously - they take it seriously.
They really care, and they work hard. It's pretty much all they do, actually.
But they've found ways.. Certain, whatever, 'hobbies,' you know, to cope.
Not great video quality: the guys interviewed on a cheap set. Ted is KNITTING some kind of thick, amorphous shape. POLISH INTERVIEWER (O.S.)
I never know. I just knit till it feels done.
It's very.. dense what you're knitting.
I call em yarn blobs. (then) Ask Bill what he does to relax. Yah.
What do you do to relax, Bill?
Ted moves his mouth - but we hear BILL'S VOICE. 24 YR OLD BILL'S VOICE COMING FROM TED I do ventriloquism, dude! CUT BACK TO: JOANNA AND ELIZABETH
How do you cope? A beat.
The women look at each other.. silence. Then:
Let's just say things didn't go the way we all expected them to go.
1995: 25 YR OLD BILL AND TED (with the princesses) rush from
the studio toward SUVs. CAMERAS PUSH IN - it's like John and Paul leaving Abbey Road with Linda and Yoko in 1968.. Bill!
Ted! When's the record coming out?
(a McCartneyesque double-barrelled finger-pointed thumbs up) Almost there!
All good things come to those who wait.
1996: STILL PHOTOS OF 26 YEAR OLD BILL AND TED BEHIND THE SCENES AT A RADIO STATION INTERVIEW. BEARDED. A COUPLE QUICK PUSH-INS - still upbeat and assured.
..So - the record? What gives? How about right here - right now - you tell us the definitive release date?
Oh.. Yeah.. Can't do that..
Just.. Changing directions a little bit. But it's all good stuff.
They lost three record deals in as many years. Cause, the thing with record deals, when you get down to it: you kind of have to make a record.
I personally felt sorry for them. They were trying so hard that they were actually driving people away..
QUICK PHOTOS: Bill and Ted playing increasingly obscure instruments and wearing increasingly eccentric clothes (capes, flight suits, kilts, knickerbockers.. play
Or, maybe as people were drifting away, it just made them try harder, it's tough to know.
QUICK VIDEO: A small venue. Bill & Ted wear loin cloths and sandals and sport long Jesus beards and blow shofars. CUT TO: KANYE WEST AND JAY Z

It's like they went from "She Loves You" to "Revolution Number Nine" in a very short time, without ever doing any of the songs in between. Or even doing "She Loves You," when you think about it. Or "Revolution Number Nine."
To be honest, they kind of just did nothing. But a very lame, very pretentious version of nothing.
SMALL OUTDOOR FESTIVAL - HAND HELD FAN VIDEO
Bill - totally nude (except for black combat boots) - does a lead guitar version of TAPS while he approaches the mic.
Let us now declare the epoch of the electric guitar.. officially dead.
We now reveal TED, behind him, also nude (and also in combat boots), with a chain saw.
(to his guitar) I sacrifice thee, obsolescence embodied.. For eternity!
Now Ted chainsaws off the neck of Bill's guitar - which sparks and feeds back.
I think that was that moment. The Riverside Metal Fest. Okay - first of all, right off the bat - they were using a chain saw nude. I mean, that tells you something about their judgement right there.
I don't think we heard another thing from them til.. Hunh.. I don't even know.
MARQUEE OUTSIDE A DIVE BAR: "Ponderosa Lounge - 8PM The Wild
Stallions - and half-priced Buffalo chicken wings." PUSH IN.
Are they even still making music? what are they even doing now?
I mean, THROUGH THE WINDOW we see a grainy image of Bill and Ted, on stage.. we FREEZE, then PULL BACK as the image becomes a: HOLOGRAM and we begin to realize we are:
INT. FUTURISTIC DOME - CONTINUOUS
Where the THREE MOST IMPORTANT PEOPLE IN THE WORLD float in mid-air, gravely looking at the image before them. Super:
San Dimas, California, 2713.
They turn to a FOURTH MAN - very severe, almost puritanical looking, dressed in all black (ROLLINS).
Their time is running out. We must take emergency measures. The future of

everything hangs in the balance. On Rollins' face, a slight flash of concern - and surprise. He steps into a SLEEK, SILVER METALLIC BOOTH.. dons his SUNGLASSES. And, with a nod to the Great Ones.. The booth shimmers, drops through the floor.
EXT. WEDDING - DAY

Bill and Ted, in their mid-40's, in suits, walk up to a microphone. Behind them, a banner reads "CONGRATULATIONS DEACON AND MISSY." We are at a wedding. Garden-variety, middle class. Joanna and Elizabeth are there (also in their mid 40's). Elizabeth is with WILL (18); Joanna is with THEO (also 18). The guys are buoyant, exuberant-- utterly unflappable.
Good afternoon. And welcome to this joyous occasion.
Ted and I have both known Missy in different capacities for nearly 40 years.
She was Bill's babysitter when we were ten, and we both invited her to the prom when she was a senior and we were freshman.
Next she married my dad, and Missy became
"Mom."
After quickly divorcing Bill's dad, Missy married my dad, and became my mom.
Ted's dad, POLICE CHIEF CHET LOGAN, now in his mid 60's and still very fit, raises his glass with a tight smile that only barely masks his bitterness.
And now she is marrying Ted's younger brother, Officer Deacon Logan.
We see DEACON now, 40, buzz cut, with MISSY, now late 40's. He raises his glass.
Deacon - my congratulations. Missy - Bill and I - along with Joanna and Elizabeth, and Will and Theo - welcome you back with open arms.
Elizabeth, Joanna, Will and Theo all raise glasses.
This joyous event would seem to make
Deacon his own father-in-law, and make Ted his own uncle.
Not to mention making my dad his own son.
In back, Logan just shakes his head, irked on so many levels.
So with that beautiful thought in mind, for your first dance, we wish to present a
sneak peek at the world premiere of our newest sonic creation..
So please enjoy the first three movements of..
"That Which Binds Us Through Time: The Chemical, Physical and Biological Nature of Love; An Exploration of the Meaning of Meaning."
Ready, Bill?
Ready, Ted. One.. Two..
One two three four..
Ted plays bagpipes. Bill is engaged in a kind of widemouthed Aboriginal 'throat-singing.' While clearly a great deal of thought (and even love) has gone into the preparation

Wedding guests watch with jaw-dropped befuddlement as - Deacon and Missy step to the dance floor - aware that this is somehow their "first dance." But it's hard for them to decipher how, exactly, they're supposed to move to this. Joanna and Elizabeth exchange a glance. As do Will and Theo. Ted's dad just openly throws his hands up..
This is bullshit.
..and crosses to a power bank and literally pulls the plug on the whole thing. CUT TO:
THIRTY SECONDS LATER
Ted and his dad, off to the side of the stage.
I'm sorry you don't get it, Dad.
No one gets it, Ted. No one ever has. There's nothing to get.
They will, Dad, seriously.

I don't think so. That song is the worst one yet. And that's saying a lot. I told Deacon he should never have let you guys play. I'm sorry, "play." And honestly on a very deep level - it is time to pull the plug. Both of you. We can't.
We have to pursue this until --
Yeah yeah yeah, I know, until your "music saves the world." Ted. Enough with your delusions.
THE VAN
Where Bill wheels equipment to the back door. He pulls it open and sees - his FATHER, BOB PRESTON, slumped in the back, having just been awakened. Hey dad.
How was the wedding?
It was good. You shoulda come in.
Weddings are hard for me, Billy. My social worker says I've never really recovered from your mom leaving all those years ago. Your real mom, not Missy. I know.
That was hard.
Mr. Preston gives Bill a huge hug.
I love you, Billy.
I know, Dad. And I love you, too. (as his dad continues to hug him) Okay, Dad. It's totally fine. Everything's good, Dad. Everything's gonna be okay. I really gotta go get some more stuff.
He breaks off, turns. And we follow him to --
Still in the thick of it.

Dad - I'm just saying: if you'd done the things me and Bill have done, and seen the things we've seen -CHIEF LOGAN No - you can keep "spinning" this however you want, Ted, but I'll never believe it. You didn't time travel. You didn't die and go to hell and then come back.
Chief Logan - we did CHIEF LOGAN No. You didn't, Bill. And here's the reason you didn't: it's impossible.
Dad - I know you're upset because Deacon just married --
No. Ted - you're wrong. It has nothing to do with that. Trust me, if I'm more disappointed in one of my sons, it's you.
Logan spins to storm away. Then turns back.
By the way? That five thousand dollars I loaned you? I want it back.
You'll get it back.

When, Ted? You're upside down on your mortgage. You're fifty thousand dollars in debt. Your wife is working two jobs so you two can pursue your pathetic fantasy. Get real, Ted. Get real - both of you.
And he's off. Ted just stands there. Ted's shoulder. Bill puts his hand on
Dude - if it's any consolation, I'm eightythousand dollars in debt.
What are we gonna do? No.
No, no, no.
INT. FABULOUS FORUM - DAY
Keys and coat in hand, the stadium manager (FRANK) closes his office door as Bill and Ted follow him out into the arena.
No, you may not have your non-refundable deposit back. But --
No 'buts.' Twenty years ago you signed a contract - put down a deposit - to rent the Forum tomorrow night for your concert celebrating "Two Decades of Greatness--"
Triumphance. Whatever.
I tell you this every year--
But this year is different.
It's quite different - as you will see if you'd simply listen to us. Fine.
Go ahead.
This year, given the direness of our financial situation, we're willing to reduce our offer from a one hundred percent refund to an eighty percent refund.
So wait - I'm sorry - then I'd only have to give you eighty thousand dollars that I contractually do not owe you rather than a hundred thousand?
Correct. But between us, our bottom line is sixty thousand, for which we will take
But no lower than twenty-five.
Of which we would accept ten.
Ted leans to Bill.
Dude - I have a sneaking feeling that we're negotiating against ourselves.
Good point, Ted. (turns) Forget everything we said. We're back at a hundred thousand. Plus interest.

Okay, then I guess I should make my counter offer: nothing. The point is: you bought the Forum tomorrow night - it's yours. Do whatever you want with it throw a party at mid-field, I don't care. The lights'll be on, the gate'll be open.
By the way - just out of curiosity - how many tickets have you guys sold?
Well, it's hard to say - it could have changed, cause we haven't checked the web site in a while.
That's fine. Just give me the ballpark
Okay - ballpark? I'm just estimating here, but approximately zero.
Frank shakes his head.
I have to tell you guys - what you have pulled off here is very impressive, in a way. You've promoted a concert for twenty years, and sold zero tickets.
Thanks, dude.
It's not a compliment.
INT. ELEVATOR - DAY
Bill and Joanna ride up in silence. They get off and enter..
INT. WAITING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
And Joanna stops as she sees: Elizabeth and Ted, also in the waiting room. Elizabeth is equally surprised. Ted is not.
How's it goin' dude?
Hey, Jo!
I'm sorry - is this the right day? Yah. But..
Totally. Couples therapy. JOANNA
Now the other door opens and the THERAPIST sticks his head out - a bit surprised to see TWO couples in his waiting room.
INT. THERAPIST'S OFFICE - A MOMENT LATER
Bill, with Joanna, and Ted, with Elizabeth, sit across from the Therapist. It's a little crowded on the couch.
So do you understand why this situation might seem at all strange to your wives?
No, not at all.
Well, when your wives suggested couples therapy, did you think this is what they had in mind?
Yah. Definitely. I mean, we're a couple of couples, right?
Well, it usually means one couple. Ohhh..
BILL/TED
But since you're here - let me ask you this. Is there something you feel your wives need to hear from you?
Totally. (turning to Elizabeth) We love you guys. Yah.
Okay, that's good. But.. Do you understand how that might sound strange to your wives? Bill? No.
We love em.
"We" love them?
Yah, we do. "We" do?
Yah, we do. Exactly.
It's the "we" part.. Ted. Can you say the same thing using "I" in the sentence?
Ohhh. Yah, of course. (turns to Elizabeth) Elizabeth. I - and Bill - love you - and Joanna.
Elizabeth just stares at the floor, shakes her head.
Dude. All due respect - I don't think you're getting this.
Go for it, dude.
Okay. (turns) Joanna. From the very very bottom of I and Ted's - hearts. I - and Ted - totally love and worship you - and Elizabeth. Bill beams.
Ted nods - as if he now sees.
Joanna, would you or Elizabeth like to respond?
Elizabeth indicates "go ahead."

Well.. Okay. I know you love me, Bill. However - and I can't speak for Elizabeth, but.. you guys are gone over two hundred nights a year. Every time you go out on tour to make money, you come back deeper in debt.

But we have to -JOANNA I know. I know it's a burden for you. And I know you feel as though you have to do it yourselves. But you don't. We literally left everything behind, our families, our friends..
We moved 600 years in the future to be with you. To be your partners. But it's like all you think about is the song..
The mission..
And it's like we and the boys are not even there. So.. Wait.
What are you saying?
I guess what I'm saying is.. If things don't change.. I don't know how much longer I can go on like this.
Ted is stunned. He looks at Bill. Bill turns to Joanna.
Do you feel the same way?
Joanna nods.
INT. DODGE NEON - DRIVING

Ted and Elizabeth, in tense silence. Ted glances over - she just stares straight ahead. They pull up to the end of -EXT. CUL DE SAC - EVENING - CONTINUOUS Where TWO HOUSES share a driveway at the end of a quiet middle-class street. As they pull in -A GEO METRO is already there, and Bill and Joanna are getting out - also with palpable tension between them. (What is clear to us now is: they all live right next door to each other, and share a driveway - and garage.) As the women head towards the Logan house, Bill and Ted join each other.
Dude - things are worse than I thought. think they're actually thinking about leaving us.
I know, it's bad. work to do.. But we still got our
They look at their wives, who are conferring at the front door.
Okay - let's just try and work sorta fast. Yah.
Totally.
As they start toward the GARAGE-- suddenly we HEAR the sound of GUITARS from within. Ted exhales heavily.
I've told Will over and over..
They pull the garage door open, revealing:
INT. GARAGE - EVENING - CONTINUOUS
Will and Theo are playing classic old guitars (the very ones that Bill and Ted played at the end of "Bogus Journey.") They immediately stop, look at their fathers.
How's it goin, dad? Out! What?
This is our studio - and those are our guitars.
He and Bill pull the guitars from their sons' hands.
We have important work to do, and we can't have you messing around in here.
You wanna at least see what we've been working on?
Will holds a piece of paper out. his pocket. Ted takes it, shoves it in
Later. But right now, we need the studio. (a beat) Now, please, Will. Okay, Grampa.
The boys shake their heads.
I told you, dude. It's always the same. Let's just go play up in my room. With our crappy guitars.
Will nods and the boys head out. Bill and Ted watch them go, guilty, as Ted shuts the garage door and we cut to:
INT. GARAGE/MUSIC STUDIO - NIGHT
Bill and Ted are working on a song; struggling. Bill is noodling on the synthesizer - Ted is scratching out lyrics.
Okay, I added a couple things. Go again.
Bill starts on the synthesizer: it's abstruse, complex (not in a good way) - like D-grade Bohemian Rhapsody.
"We try - y - y - y to carry--yy.. on. Ayiii-eee-ee-ee-ee..a-ooo-oo-oo-oo."
Ted stops. Bill picks it up.
"OoEEEE-eeee-YIYIYIYI.." (to Ted) Dude - run with me. I think we're onto something.. (resuming)
"We straaa-aaaa-aaa-aaa-aain..yiyi-yi-yi-"
Dude - seriously. Stop. I hate to say this, but we're NOT onto anything.
What are you talking about? our best one yet.
I think it's
Dude - if that's true - then things are even worse than I thought. Cause this song sucks. You do know that, right?
I don't agree. Dude.
No.

Bill - come on. It's me. You don't have to spin me. It sucks. All our songs suck. What are we doing? Seriously. Our wives are mad at us, our sons are mad at us, we're broke-- why are we even doing this?
We're doing it cause we have to! Ted we're supposed to save all humanity. Says who??
Those people - from the future -TED What if they were wrong?
How could they be wrong? future - we were there.
We saw the
Alls I know is, at this moment, I feel like for the last 20 years we've been beating our heads against a wall, and it hurts, and I wanna stop.
But we can't! We promised!
Who's gonna care? Seriously. I'm sick of it. And you know you are too.
Bill just looks at Ted - for a long moment - and then he just deflates, dropping the facade.
Okay. Yah. I'm totally sick of it. But I thought if I ever admitted it - to you or me - we'd completely crumble. There's a pause.
I can't do this any more. I just can't.

Another pause. Finally Bill looks at Ted.. And nods, agreeing. Then, suddenly, there's -- A FLASH of LIGHT outside, visible under the garage door.. Then: We HEAR a metallic WHOOSH.. Then FOOTSTEPS. Or something.. is outside. Someone.. Bill and Ted look at each other, walk toward the garage door and slowly open it, revealing-The futuristic BOOTH. Whoa..
BILL/TED
And next to the booth, emerging from its shadow, ROLLINS.
Who are you?
Come with me, please.
Are you from the future? Yes.
A beat.
The guys look at each other again.
Are you mad at us?
We have very little time.
He opens the door for them. Ted leans closer to Bill.
I think we're in trouble.
Relax, Ted. They totally love us in the future.
The guys get into the sleek, elegant booth. Rollins closes the door, and in an instant.. It shimmers.. and DISAPPEARS..
INT. CIRCUITS OF TIME
Dark. Misty. Glimpses of light. silver booth soars past in blur. We hear a WHOOOSH and the
INT. BOOTH - ON BILL AND TED
Bill and Ted - stunned, amazed - gape out at the circuits of time.. for the first time in a long time. And we cut to:
INT. GREAT DOME - THE YEAR 2712 - DAY
The booth appears and touches down on the floor. Rollins gets out, followed by Bill and Ted. Bill and Ted whisper: Dude.
BILL
I think I liked Rufus better.
Me too. (turns to Rollins) What is it you do here, dude?
I clean up messes.

The guys look at each other as they hear.. BOOMING VOICE Bill and Ted. Step closer please. Now they turn and see: THREE IMPOSING FIGURES-- who look at Bill and Ted with barely restrained urgency and frustration. They are fierce, unblinking. It's dark and intense. and Ted look at each other, swallow.
Around the room is a kind of "Senate" of observers who line the walls. Bill adopts an upbeat manner.
How's it goin'? Lookin' great. Good to be back again.
What do you have to say for yourselves? Oh..
They look at each other, then adopt their arm-raised posture.
Be excellent to each other..?
And.. party on, dudes..? Dead silence.
No smiles. No nods. Nothing but scowls.
Where's the song?
The guys eyes widen..
Oh.. The song. Which one?
We've written so many.
The song. The song that will fulfill your destiny. The one that is to be played at the Fabulous Forum tomorrow night. The one that will save the world as we know it.
Oh, yah. That song. that song. Yah. We definitely have
The Leader makes a gesture. Lights come up and suddenly all around them are MUSICAL INSTRUMENTS of all shapes and sizes.
Then play it.
Like right now?
We need to hear it.
Um.. Before we do that -BILL Which we very much look forward to doing -TED And are most eminently prepared to do -BILL Being as it's already written --
And is very good on the level of lyrics, and melody, and rhythmic aspects as well as meter and, you know, thematic content.. we need to.. Tweak.. Yah.
Tweak..
Or, even better, perhaps what would expedite this entire process..
Would be if you played the song for us right now.
Or perhaps played it several times.
Or even just give us a recording. So we may be certain we have the exact rendition of our greatest song ever written..
A pause.
You don't have it. What?!
No. Just the opposite.

In fact, our problem is we have so many great songs that as we put together our set list for the concert tomorrow night -BILL The one we in no way bailed on years ago -TED And which as recently as an hour ago did not try to get our money back from BILL Right, that did not happen..
You're not watching us all the time, are you?
Silence. Then the Leader just waves his arms and THE FLOATING GREAT FIGURES DISAPPEAR INTO DARKNESS -- as do the instruments, and instantly-Rollins is standing right in front of the guys.
The window of destiny is closing. You have twenty four hours to come up with the song.
But-- What if for some reason we don't?
Which would be most paradoxical because obviously we do already have it.
But, you know, just theoretically.. cause we are intrigued with all manner of thought experiment involving, you know, "the conundrum of time"..
So-- What would happen?
Rollins looks at them.
Come with me.
He turns. The guys look at each other, then follow him as two huge doors slide open and they all enter -A CATHEDRAL-LIKE CHAMBER Built solely to house:
A HOLOGRAPHIC TIME-LINE
Ethereal, majestic, floating in mid-air. Weaving and winding from the deepest past all the way to the present - and, still, moment by moment, continuing to inch forward. Whoa... BILL/TED
What is this?
This is time. It's the path through which the past connects with the present.. and leads to the future.

The guys move closer. Now they can see that the "weave" is actually a collection of INFINITESIMALLY SMALL BITS OF INFORMATION. Rollins reaches out and TOUCHES a section, in the way one might touch, say, an iPad. As he does..

..an IMAGE FROM 1945 balloons out: Hiroshima. He removes his finger - the image recedes. He touches another spot.. ROLLINS (CONT'D) ..and out balloons an image of the Man standing in front of the tank in Tiananmen Square.

Time is more fluid, and more contingent, than you understand. There are key moments in the weave - turning points like stitches, if you want to think of them that way. And when one is missing, then everything can fray.
Has that ever happened?
It's about to happen. Meaning..?
If you don't have the song in 24 hours, time as we all know it will essentially unravel. Forward, and backward. Oh...
EXT. CUL DE SAC - HOUSE - NIGHT
The booth appears. The guys get out, still in shock. Rollins gives Ted a TIMEPIECE of some sort.. like a really cool, sleek WATCH.
Keep a close eye on this. You have 24 hours. When it runs out, it's over.
Rollins crosses back to his booth and gets in.. And, with a nod at them, he's gone.
Okay - bad news: the whole unraveling of time thing could be most heinous. Good news: This means there is a song. Otherwise they wouldn't be so mad at us for not having it. ..Yah.
I think that's probably right.
So now, alls we gotta do is stop writing songs that totally suck, and just write the greatest song ever written. Okay?
..Yah.. I guess.
Bill starts into the garage. Ted remains on the curb.
Ted, what are you doing, dude? keep going. We gotta
I just don't get it.. I mean-- We've been trying for 20 years to write the greatest song ever written. How are we gonna do it in 24 hours?
I don't know.. But we gotta. (beat) I mean.. Right? What else are we gonna do, dude?
Ted nods - distant and distracted.
But we do know the song's gonna exist, right?
Well, yah.. at some point..
I just wish there was a way that we could go into the future and get it from ourselves.
How would we do that?
There's a pause. They look at each other, sharing a slowly dawning realization.
Could we actually...?
Couldn't we..?
But-- didn't Rufus say we could only do that in an extreme emergency?
Bill-- if the future of all of reality as we know it is at stake.. isn't that an extreme emergency?
EXT. BILL'S HOUSE - NIGHT - MOMENT LATER
The guys head towards a STORAGE SHED at the very back of the property. At the shed, they undo a series of heavy metal padlocks. The door opens.. a single light bulb is switched on..
INT. SHED - CONTINUOUS

The guys walk to the darkest, most shadowy corner and start to pull a series of covers, sheets, tarps, etc., off of what gradually reveals itself as.. THE PHONE BOOTH.. The old one from the earlier movies. It's dusty, rusted, obviously hasn't been used in 20 years.
Okay, so here's the plan: We go into the future to when we've actually written the song, find ourselves, and take it.
One question: Is that stealing?
Why would it be stealing if we're getting it from ourselves?
Oh, yah.. (eyeing the booth) You think it still works?
Only one way to find out, dude..
INT. PHONE BOOTH - SECONDS LATER
They get in. Dust off the key pad.
How far into the future do you think we need to go?
Depends on when we think we're gonna write the song.
I say definitely within the next year or
Well, let's be conservative. years?
Sounds good. (a pause) You wanna do the honors, or should I?
Go for it, dude.
Ted - with not a little bit of nostalgia and a good deal of trepidation.. starts to hit the buttons.
